01_initial_request.input_data: Bu Lian Ta (BLT) bituminous coal, Yang Quan (YQ), Lu An (LA), and Jiao Zuo (JZ) anthracites
01_initial_request.user_intent: Improve the high temperature melting characteristics of bituminous coal with low ash melting point to meet blast furnace injection requirements
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Ash melting point temperatures (deformation, softening, hemispherical, flow) exceeding 1,533 K

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] BLT bituminous coal has low ash melting point (softening temperature 1,503 K) due to high CaO and Fe2O3 content. [Gap] Need to characterize the base materials before blending. [Decision] To achieve material characterization, chose to use industrial analysis and elemental analysis methods on BLT bituminous coal and three anthracites (YQ, LA, JZ), expecting to obtain composition data for blending design.

parameters.coal_types: BLT bituminous coal, YQ anthracite, LA anthracite, JZ anthracite

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Industrial analysis and elemental analysis results obtained for four pulverized coal ashes (shown in Table 1)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Base coal materials have been characterized. [Gap] Need to create blended coal samples with different anthracite proportions. [Decision] To achieve coal blending, chose to use manual mixing method to combine anthracite with BLT bituminous coal at four ratios (5:5, 6:4, 7:3, 8:2), expecting to obtain blended coal samples with controlled volatile fraction (11-20%).
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Blended coal samples created with volatile fraction controlled between 11 and 20%, BLT bituminous coal ash percentage distributed between 13 and 45%

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Blended coal samples have been prepared. [Gap] Need to convert coal to ash for melting characteristic testing. [Decision] To achieve ash preparation, chose to use muffle furnace to perform complete ashing at 1,088 K, expecting to obtain ash samples for melting point measurement.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ash samples obtained from different proportions of pulverized coal after complete ashing

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ash samples have been prepared from blended coal. [Gap] Need to measure ash melting characteristics (DT, ST, HT, FT). [Decision] To achieve ash melting measurement, chose to use melting point and rate measuring instrument to test ash samples from 1,273 to 1,673 K, expecting to obtain deformation, softening, hemispherical, and flow temperatures.

tool.name: Melting point and rate measuring instrument

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ash melting characteristic temperatures automatically recorded; minimum softening temperature higher than 1,533 K for all blended coals; some samples recorded as >1,673 K

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ash melting characteristics have been measured experimentally. [Gap] Need to analyze chemical composition of ash to understand melting behavior. [Decision] To achieve composition analysis, chose to use X-ray fluorescence to analyze chemical composition of four kinds of pulverized coal ashes, expecting to obtain oxide content data (SiO2, Al2O3, Fe2O3, CaO).

parameters.sample_types: BLT bituminous coal ash, YQ anthracite ash, LA anthracite ash, JZ anthracite ash

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Chemical composition of ash analyzed; main oxides identified as SiO2, Al2O3, Fe2O3, and CaO (shown in Table 2 and Table 4)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Experimental ash melting data and chemical composition have been obtained. [Gap] Need to calculate complete melting temperature using thermodynamic software for prediction model. [Decision] To achieve thermodynamic calculation, chose to use FactSage thermodynamic calculation software with FTOxid database to calculate complete melting temperature of CaO-SiO2-Al2O3-Fe2O3 quaternary system, expecting to obtain liquidus temperature values.

parameters.liquid_phase_setting: FToxid-SLAGA set to P option (Precipitate target phase)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Complete melting temperature values calculated for single coal and mixed coal ash samples (shown in Table 6)

references.citation: Li, D. X., 2012, Master's thesis, East China University of Science and Technology
references.relevance: Previous study found ash liquid temperature can be used to predict ash melting temperature with linear relationship

references.citation: Shu, H. N., 2006, Master dissertation, Zhejiang University
references.relevance: Used FactSage Software to study effects of Ca and Si on melting characteristics of coal ash

references.citation: Song, W. J., 2011, Doctoral dissertation, East China University of Science and Technology
references.relevance: Used FactSage Software to study effects of CaO, Fe2O3, and MgO on melting characteristics of coal ash

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Both experimental ash melting temperatures and FactSage calculated complete melting temperatures have been obtained. [Gap] Need to establish predictive relationship between calculated and experimental values. [Decision] To achieve prediction model, chose to use linear regression analysis on softening temperature (ST) and complete melting temperature (TC), excluding data >1,673 K, expecting to obtain correlation formula for ash melting point prediction.

parameters.independent_variable: T_C (complete melting temperature calculated by software)
parameters.excluded_data: Four groups with experimental values >1,673 K (YQ, JZ, LA, A4)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Relationship obtained: T_ST = 0.7098 T_C + 257.98, indicating certain relationship between ash melting point and complete ash melting temperature

references.citation: Li, D. X., 2012, Master's thesis, East China University of Science and Technology
references.relevance: Previous linear relationship formula: T_M = 0.75 T_C + 216.25, used for comparison

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Ash melting characteristic measurement using melting point and rate measuring instrument with temperature range 1,273-1,673 K

softening_temperature_improvement_YQ_5_5.interpretation: Ash melting point increase compared to BLT bituminous coal at 5:5 ratio with YQ anthracite

softening_temperature_improvement_YQ_6_4.interpretation: Ash melting point increase compared to BLT bituminous coal at 6:4 ratio with YQ anthracite

softening_temperature_improvement_YQ_7_3.interpretation: Ash melting point increase compared to BLT bituminous coal at 7:3 ratio with YQ anthracite

softening_temperature_improvement_YQ_8_2.interpretation: Ash melting point increase compared to BLT bituminous coal at 8:2 ratio with YQ anthracite

minimum_softening_temperature.interpretation: All blended coals met minimum requirements for blast furnace injection (target was 1,533 K)

best_performing_anthracite.interpretation: Yang Quan anthracite had most significant effect on improving ash melting characteristics

prediction_formula.interpretation: Linear relationship between experimental softening temperature and FactSage calculated complete melting temperature

03_success_verification.final_verdict: SUCCESS - The research objective was achieved. All blended coals met the minimum ash melting point requirement of 1,533 K for blast furnace injection. The softening temperature of blended coal increased by 98-170 K depending on anthracite type and ratio. YQ anthracite showed the best improvement effect. Additionally, a predictive model (T_ST = 0.7098 T_C + 257.98) was established using FactSage software to efficiently predict coal ash melting temperature without cumbersome experiments.
